attempt: Move from bounded stateful certificates to global compactness pressure: finite decreasing quantities matter only if they can be upgraded into a theorem that rules out all infinite obstruction paths.
obstruction: A parity selector state machine still needs a compactness theorem preventing exact-gap mass from leaking into wider gaps.
candidate_theorem: A global parity compactness theorem keeps exact gap-2 mass positive at arbitrarily large scale.
next_experiment: Stress exact-gap selector states against wider-gap leakage and parity countermodels.
claim_boundary: No twin-prime proof and no certified twin-prime counterexample.
